1.name: Negroni
1.recipe:
Gin: 1 oz
Campari: 1 oz
Sweet Vermouth: 1 oz
Orange slice or orange peel
1.method: Pour gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth into a mixing glass with ice. Stir well and strain into an old-fashioned glass filled with ice. Garnish with an orange slice or orange peel.
1.description: The Negroni offers a perfect balance of bitterness and sweetness, with a layered flavor and a vibrant orange-red hue—an all-time classic.

2.name: Martini
2.recipe:
Gin: 2 oz
Dry Vermouth: 0.5 oz
Olive or lemon twist
2.method: Combine gin and dry vermouth in a mixing glass with ice, stir well, and strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with an olive or lemon twist.
2.description: The Martini is the quintessential classic cocktail—clean, crisp, and aromatic, highlighting the botanicals of gin.

3.name: Sazerac
3.recipe:
Rye whiskey or cognac: 2 oz
Absinthe: a few drops (to rinse the glass)
Sugar cube: 1
Angostura bitters: 2 dashes
Lemon peel
3.method: Rinse a chilled glass with absinthe and discard the excess. Muddle the sugar cube with bitters, add whiskey and ice, and stir well. Strain into the prepared glass and garnish with a lemon peel.
3.description: The Sazerac is one of America’s oldest cocktails, rich and aromatic with hints of spice and herbs, offering a smooth, lingering finish.

4.name: Bloody Mary
4.recipe:
Vodka: 1.5 oz
Tomato juice: 3 oz
Lemon juice: 0.5 oz
Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, salt, black pepper: to taste
Celery stick or lemon wedge for garnish
4.method: Roll all ingredients with ice in a shaker (do not shake vigorously). Pour into a highball glass and garnish with a celery stick or lemon wedge.
4.description: The Bloody Mary is savory, spicy, and full-bodied—a brunch staple that perfectly combines vegetable and alcohol flavors.

5.name: Manhattan
5.recipe:
Rye whiskey: 2 oz
Sweet Vermouth: 1 oz
Angostura bitters: 2 dashes
Cherry for garnish
5.method: Combine whiskey, sweet vermouth, and bitters in a mixing glass with ice. Stir well, then strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a cherry.
5.description: The Manhattan is smooth and balanced, with a mix of spice and sweetness—a timeless whiskey-based classic.

6.name: Americano
6.recipe:
Campari: 1 oz
Sweet Vermouth: 1 oz
Soda water: to top
Orange or lemon slice
6.method: Pour Campari and sweet vermouth into a highball glass filled with ice. Top with soda water and gently stir. Garnish with an orange or lemon slice.
6.description: The Americano is light, refreshing, and mildly bitter, with a sparkling texture—perfect as an aperitif on a warm day.

7.name: Espresso Martini
7.recipe:
Vodka: 1.5 oz
Coffee liqueur (e.g., Kahlúa): 1 oz
Fresh espresso: 1 oz
Simple syrup: 0.25 oz
Coffee beans for garnish
7.method: Shake all ingredients vigorously with ice, then strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with coffee beans.
7.description: The Espresso Martini delivers a smooth, rich mix of coffee and sweetness—a modern favorite for both coffee and cocktail lovers.

8.name: Cosmopolitan
8.recipe:
Vodka: 1.5 oz
Triple Sec: 1 oz
Cranberry juice: 0.5 oz
Fresh lime juice: 0.25 oz
Orange peel for garnish
8.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with an orange peel.
8.description: The Cosmopolitan is bright, fruity, and slightly tart, known for its elegant pink color and refreshing citrus aroma.

9.name: Bellini
9.recipe:
White peach purée: 2 oz
Prosecco: 4 oz
9.method: Pour white peach purée into a chilled flute glass, then gently top with Prosecco. Stir lightly to combine.
9.description: The Bellini is light, fruity, and refreshing, combining the sweetness of peach with sparkling Prosecco—perfect for brunch or celebrations.

10.name: Old Fashioned
10.recipe:
Bourbon or rye whiskey: 2 oz
Sugar cube: 1
Angostura bitters: 2 dashes
Water: a splash
Orange peel for garnish
10.method: Muddle the sugar cube with bitters and water in an old-fashioned glass. Add whiskey and ice, stir well, and garnish with an orange peel.
10.description: The Old Fashioned is smooth, balanced, and aromatic, highlighting the whiskey’s rich flavor with subtle sweetness and spice.

11.name: Margarita
11.recipe:
Tequila: 2 oz
Triple Sec: 1 oz
Fresh lime juice: 1 oz
Salt for rim
Lime wheel for garnish
11.method: Rim a glass with salt. Shake tequila, triple sec, and lime juice with ice, then strain into the glass. Garnish with a lime wheel.
11.description: The Margarita is tangy, refreshing, and perfectly balanced between sweet, sour, and salty—an iconic tequila cocktail.

12.name: Aperol Spritz
12.recipe:
Aperol: 2 oz
Prosecco: 3 oz
Soda water: 1 oz
Orange slice for garnish
12.method: Pour Aperol, Prosecco, and soda water into a wine glass filled with ice. Stir gently and garnish with an orange slice.
12.description: The Aperol Spritz is vibrant and bubbly, offering a bittersweet citrus flavor that’s light and refreshing—ideal for summer evenings.

13.name: Casino
13.recipe:
Old Tom Gin: 2 oz
Maraschino liqueur: 0.25 oz
Orange bitters: 1 dash
Fresh lemon juice: 0.25 oz
Cherry or lemon twist for garnish
13.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a cherry or lemon twist.
13.description: The Casino is a delicate and aromatic gin cocktail with a subtle sweetness and a crisp citrus finish.

14.name: Clover Club
14.recipe:
Gin: 1.5 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.5 oz
Raspberry syrup: 0.5 oz
Egg white: optional
Raspberries for garnish
14.method: Shake all ingredients without ice to emulsify, then add ice and shake again. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass and garnish with raspberries.
14.description: The Clover Club is elegant and smooth, featuring a bright pink color and a balance of tart lemon with sweet raspberry.

15.name: Angel Face
15.recipe:
Gin: 1 oz
Calvados: 1 oz
Apricot brandy: 1 oz
15.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
15.description: The Angel Face offers a warm, fruity flavor from apple brandy and apricot liqueur, balanced by the dryness of gin.

16.name: Aviation
16.recipe:
Gin: 1.5 oz
Maraschino liqueur: 0.5 oz
Crème de Violette: 0.25 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.5 oz
Cherry or lemon twist for garnish
16.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a cherry or lemon twist.
16.description: The Aviation is floral and slightly tart, with a pale violet hue and a unique balance of citrus and cherry flavors.

17.name: Between the Sheets
17.recipe:
Cognac: 1 oz
White rum: 1 oz
Triple Sec: 0.5 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.5 oz
17.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
17.description: Between the Sheets is a smooth and citrusy cocktail that blends rum and cognac for a rich yet refreshing flavor.

18.name: Cosmopolitan
18.recipe:
Vodka: 1.5 oz
Triple Sec: 1 oz
Cranberry juice: 0.5 oz
Fresh lime juice: 0.25 oz
Orange peel for garnish
18.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with an orange peel.
18.description: The Cosmopolitan is fruity and slightly tart with a beautiful pink hue, offering a perfect balance of citrus and sweetness.

19.name: Derby
19.recipe:
Bourbon: 2 oz
Sweet Vermouth: 0.5 oz
Orange curaçao: 0.25 oz
Fresh lime juice: 0.25 oz
Bitters: 1 dash
Mint sprig for garnish
19.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with a mint sprig.
19.description: The Derby is a complex bourbon-based cocktail with herbal and citrus notes, refreshing yet sophisticated.

20.name: Lemon Drop Martini
20.recipe:
Vodka: 2 oz
Triple Sec: 0.5 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.75 oz
Simple syrup: 0.25 oz
Sugar for rim
Lemon twist for garnish
20.method: Rim a martini glass with sugar. Shake vodka, triple sec, lemon juice, and syrup with ice, then strain into the prepared glass. Garnish with a lemon twist.
20.description: The Lemon Drop Martini is zesty and bright, combining tangy citrus with a hint of sweetness—both elegant and uplifting.

21.name: Hemingway Special
21.recipe:
White rum: 2 oz
Fresh grapefruit juice: 0.75 oz
Maraschino liqueur: 0.25 oz
Fresh lime juice: 0.5 oz
21.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
21.description: The Hemingway Special is crisp and tart, a rum cocktail with citrus brightness and a light cherry finish, inspired by the famous writer himself.

22.name: Margarita
22.recipe:
Tequila: 2 oz
Triple Sec: 1 oz
Fresh lime juice: 1 oz
Salt for rim
Lime wheel for garnish
22.method: Rim a glass with salt. Shake tequila, triple sec, and lime juice with ice, then strain into the glass. Garnish with a lime wheel.
22.description: The Margarita is tangy, refreshing, and perfectly balanced between sweet, sour, and salty—an iconic tequila cocktail.

23.name: Mary Pickford
23.recipe:
White rum: 1.5 oz
Pineapple juice: 1.5 oz
Grenadine: 0.25 oz
Maraschino liqueur: 0.25 oz
Cherry for garnish
23.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a cherry.
23.description: The Mary Pickford is sweet and tropical, with a pink hue and fruity blend of pineapple and cherry flavors.

24.name: Kamikaze
24.recipe:
Vodka: 1 oz
Triple Sec: 1 oz
Fresh lime juice: 1 oz
Lime wedge for garnish
24.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a lime wedge.
24.description: The Kamikaze is bold and tangy, offering a clean citrus punch with a smooth vodka base—simple yet satisfying.

25.name: Whiskey Sour
25.recipe:
Bourbon: 2 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.75 oz
Simple syrup: 0.75 oz
Egg white: optional
Cherry or orange slice for garnish
25.method: Shake all ingredients with ice (dry shake first if using egg white), then strain into an old-fashioned glass filled with ice. Garnish with a cherry or orange slice.
25.description: The Whiskey Sour is a perfect balance of sweet and sour, smooth and refreshing, with a rich citrus aroma.

26.name: Mojito
26.recipe:
White rum: 2 oz
Fresh lime juice: 0.75 oz
Simple syrup: 0.5 oz
Mint leaves: 6–8
Soda water: to top
Mint sprig for garnish
26.method: Muddle mint leaves with lime juice and syrup in a glass. Add rum and ice, top with soda water, and gently stir. Garnish with a mint sprig.
26.description: The Mojito is light, refreshing, and aromatic, combining citrus, mint, and rum in a bubbly, crisp cocktail.

27.name: The Flip
27.recipe:
Brandy or rum: 1.5 oz
Simple syrup: 0.25 oz
Whole egg: 1
Nutmeg for garnish
27.method: Shake all ingredients vigorously without ice, then add ice and shake again. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass and garnish with freshly grated nutmeg.
27.description: The Flip is rich and creamy with a velvety texture, subtly sweet and nutty, perfect as a dessert cocktail.

28.name: Negroni
28.recipe:
Gin: 1 oz
Campari: 1 oz
Sweet Vermouth: 1 oz
Orange slice or peel for garnish
28.method: Pour gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth into a mixing glass with ice. Stir well and strain into an old-fashioned glass filled with ice. Garnish with an orange slice or peel.
28.description: The Negroni is bitter-sweet with a layered flavor and a vivid orange-red hue—an all-time classic.

29.name: Espresso Martini
29.recipe:
Vodka: 1.5 oz
Coffee liqueur: 1 oz
Fresh espresso: 1 oz
Simple syrup: 0.25 oz
Coffee beans for garnish
29.method: Shake all ingredients vigorously with ice, then strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with coffee beans.
29.description: Rich and smooth, the Espresso Martini blends coffee and sweetness for a modern favorite.

30.name: Daiquiri
30.recipe:
White rum: 2 oz
Fresh lime juice: 1 oz
Simple syrup: 0.5 oz
Lime wheel for garnish
30.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a lime wheel.
30.description: The Daiquiri is crisp, citrusy, and perfectly balanced—a classic rum cocktail that is refreshing and simple.

31.name: Manhattan
31.recipe:
Rye whiskey: 2 oz
Sweet Vermouth: 1 oz
Angostura bitters: 2 dashes
Cherry for garnish
31.method: Combine whiskey, sweet vermouth, and bitters in a mixing glass with ice. Stir well and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a cherry.
31.description: Smooth and balanced, the Manhattan features a mix of spice and sweetness—a timeless whiskey cocktail.

32.name: Cosmopolitan
32.recipe:
Vodka: 1.5 oz
Triple Sec: 1 oz
Cranberry juice: 0.5 oz
Fresh lime juice: 0.25 oz
Orange peel for garnish
32.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with an orange peel.
32.description: Bright, fruity, and slightly tart, the Cosmopolitan is elegant with a refreshing citrus aroma.

33.name: Margarita
33.recipe:
Tequila: 2 oz
Triple Sec: 1 oz
Fresh lime juice: 1 oz
Salt for rim
Lime wheel for garnish
33.method: Rim a glass with salt. Shake tequila, triple sec, and lime juice with ice, then strain into the glass. Garnish with a lime wheel.
33.description: Tangy, refreshing, and perfectly balanced between sweet, sour, and salty—an iconic tequila cocktail.

34.name: Blue Curacao Cocktail
34.recipe:
Vodka: 1.5 oz
Blue Curacao: 1 oz
Lemon juice: 0.5 oz
Simple syrup: 0.25 oz
Lemon slice for garnish
34.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a lemon slice.
34.description: Bright blue and visually stunning, with a sweet and citrusy flavor profile.

35.name: Highball
35.recipe:
Whiskey: 2 oz
Ginger ale or soda water: to top
Lemon wedge for garnish
35.method: Fill a highball glass with ice, pour whiskey, top with ginger ale or soda water, and garnish with a lemon wedge.
35.description: Simple, light, and refreshing, highlighting the whiskey with subtle carbonation.

36.name: Sidecar
36.recipe:
Cognac: 2 oz
Triple Sec: 1 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.75 oz
Sugar for rim
Lemon twist for garnish
36.method: Rim the glass with sugar. Shake cognac, triple sec, and lemon juice with ice, strain into the glass. Garnish with a lemon twist.
36.description: Citrusy, slightly sweet, and balanced, a timeless cognac cocktail.

37.name: Amy and Navy
37.recipe:
Gin: 2 oz
Apricot brandy: 0.5 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.5 oz
Sugar syrup: 0.25 oz
37.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
37.description: Fruity, tart, and balanced, combining gin’s dryness with apricot sweetness.

38.name: Aviation
38.recipe:
Gin: 1.5 oz
Maraschino liqueur: 0.5 oz
Crème de Violette: 0.25 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.5 oz
Cherry for garnish
38.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a cherry.
38.description: Floral and slightly tart, pale violet in color, with a unique balance of citrus and cherry flavors.

39.name: Bees Knees
39.recipe:
Gin: 2 oz
Fresh lemon juice: 0.75 oz
Honey syrup: 0.5 oz
Lemon twist for garnish
39.method: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a lemon twist.
39.description: Smooth, citrusy, and lightly sweet, with honey adding a natural richness to the gin base.

40.name: Paloma
40.recipe:
Tequila: 2 oz
Fresh grapefruit juice: 2 oz
Simple syrup: 0.5 oz
Soda water: to top
Lime wedge for garnish
40.method: Fill a highball glass with ice, add tequila, grapefruit juice, and syrup. Top with soda water and garnish with a lime wedge.
40.description: Crisp, refreshing, and slightly bitter, a perfect tequila-based cocktail for sunny days.

41.name: 50 50 Martini
41.recipe:
Gin: 1.5 oz
Dry Vermouth: 1.5 oz
Lemon twist or olive
41.method: Add gin and dry vermouth into a mixing glass with plenty of ice. Stir until well chilled, then strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with a lemon twist or olive.
41.description: The 50/50 Martini is a lighter, more approachable variation of the classic martini, offering a clean and balanced profile that highlights both the gin and dry vermouth.

42.name: Blood and Sand
42.recipe:
Scotch: 0.75 oz
Sweet Vermouth: 0.75 oz
Orange juice: 0.75 oz
Cherry Heering: 0.75 oz
Orange peel or cherry
42.method: Add scotch, sweet vermouth, orange juice, and Cherry Heering into a shaker with ice. Shake until well chilled and strain into a coupe or cocktail glass. Garnish with an orange peel or a cherry.
42.description: Blood and Sand is a classic whisky cocktail known for its rich balance of smoky scotch, bright orange, and deep cherry sweetness. Its distinctive orange-red hue and smooth, rounded flavor make it one of the few enduring Scotch-based classics.

43.name: Black Russian
43.method: Pour vodka and coffee liqueur into an old-fashioned glass filled with ice. Stir gently.
43.description: The Black Russian is a simple yet bold cocktail combining the clean bite of vodka with the deep, sweet bitterness of coffee liqueur.

44.name: Blackthorn
44.recipe:
Irish Whiskey: 2 oz
Dry Vermouth: 1 oz
Bitters: 2 dashes
Lemon twist
44.method: Add whiskey, dry vermouth, and bitters into a mixing glass with ice. Stir until chilled and strain into a coupe glass. Garnish with a lemon twist.
44.description: The Blackthorn is a refined, aromatic cocktail featuring Irish whiskey and dry vermouth, offering a crisp herbal character with a smooth finish.

45.name: Brainstorm
45.recipe:
Irish Whiskey: 2 oz
Dry Vermouth: 0.5 oz
Benedictine: 0.5 oz
Lemon twist
45.method: Combine whiskey, dry vermouth, and Benedictine in a mixing glass with ice. Stir well and strain into a chilled coupe or martini glass. Garnish with a lemon twist.
45.description: The Brainstorm is a smooth, aromatic cocktail with layered herbal sweetness from Benedictine balanced by the roundness of Irish whiskey.

46.name: Bramble
46.recipe:
Gin: 2 oz
Lemon juice: 1 oz
Simple syrup: 0.5 oz
Crème de mûre: 0.5 oz
Fresh blackberry or lemon slice
46.method: Shake gin, lemon juice, and simple syrup with ice. Strain into an old-fashioned glass filled with crushed ice. Drizzle crème de mûre on top and garnish with a blackberry or lemon slice.
46.description: The Bramble is a modern classic featuring bright citrus and sweet blackberry flavors, refreshing and visually striking.

47.name: Caipirinha
47.recipe:
Cachaça: 2 oz
Lime: 1 (cut into wedges)
Sugar: 2 teaspoons
47.method: Muddle lime wedges and sugar in an old-fashioned glass to release the lime juice and oils. Fill the glass with crushed ice, add cachaça, and stir well.
47.description: The Caipirinha is Brazil’s national cocktail, known for its bright lime aroma, raw sugar sweetness, and the rustic, grassy character of cachaça.

48.name: Clover Club
48.recipe:
Gin: 1.5 oz
Lemon juice: 0.75 oz
Raspberry syrup: 0.5 oz
Egg white: 1
Fresh raspberry or lemon twist
48.method: Add all ingredients into a shaker without ice and dry-shake to emulsify. Add ice, shake again until well chilled, and strain into a coupe glass. Garnish with a raspberry or lemon twist.
48.description: The Clover Club is a pre-Prohibition classic, featuring a silky texture from egg white and a delicate balance of tart lemon and sweet raspberry.
